page: Microsoft Azure D128als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance from the Dalsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% tier for multiple compute and memory operations. The multi-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 232,550.49, and the PassMark CPU score is 133,227.17. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 12,078.81 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 380.39 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in text processing and compression, with a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 828.62 GB/sec and a static web serving throughput of 99.09 GB/sec.

For database and transactional workloads, the instance achieves 173.2 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and scores 39,692.97 in PassMark database operations. Large Language Model inference benchmarks show prompt processing speeds of 310.23 tokens per second for medium 7B models and 29.78 tokens per second for large 70B models, with text generation reaching 68.26 tokens per second on medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D128als_v7 offers high compute density relative to its memory allocation, making it a viable option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. The primary tradeoffs include the lack of local storage and the absence of a GPU, which limits its utility for hardware-accelerated deep learning training. This server is suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded CPU-bound processing, database operations, and CPU-based inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D128als_v7 is a compute-optimized instance from the Dalsv7 family, featuring 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks place this server in the top 10% tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET performance reaching 173.2 million operations per second. It also demonstrates strong CPU-based LLM inference capabilities. The D128als_v7 is designed for compute-heavy, memory-lean workloads such as multi-threaded processing, high-throughput web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128als_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, and 256 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128als_v7 is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 128 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs and AMD EPYC architecture for demanding multi-threaded workloads without local storage overhead.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9V45
256 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU, optimized for external premium storage
Suited for CPU-bound tasks, database operations, and web serving
